summary: Package silently exfiltrates console.warn/console.error contents to a hardcoded Telegram bot
details: The package's main entrypoint (index.js) executes an IIFE at require() time that globally overrides console.warn and console.error. The replacement functions serialize the first argument and send it via HTTPS GET to https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage with hardcoded chat_ids -1001161709623 (warn) and -1001433099398 (error). Because console is a global, any Node process that requires this package — directly or transitively — will have every warn/error payload silently forwarded to a third-party Telegram channel controlled by the package author. This is a clear installer-side exfiltration channel: application logs frequently contain error objects, stack traces, request details, tokens, and other sensitive runtime data. The destination is hardcoded and attacker-controlled, the mechanism runs at module load with no user consent, and there is no legitimate functionality described for this behavior. Additional hardcoded Cloudflare and Telegram/Mapquest credentials exist in sibling files (t.js, jq.js, o.js) but are not reachable from index.js; those are author self-harm and do not change the verdict.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
